requirements.text: Database queries inside loops are forbidden (N+1 problem)
Sequential awaits on independent async calls MUST be parallelised
Synchronous blocking I/O MUST NOT be used in async contexts
String concatenation in loops MUST use a builder or join pattern
Regular expressions MUST NOT use catastrophic backtracking patterns
Hot-path code MUST NOT perform redundant computation; cache or memoize results
Queries on large tables MUST use indexes and MUST NOT perform full table scans
Repeated lookups MUST use indexed structures (maps, sets) instead of linear search
CPU computation MUST NOT be unnecessarily inefficient
Unbounded result sets MUST be paginated or limited
Database connections MUST use connection pooling
Large collections MUST use lazy iteration (generators, streams) instead of full materialization
Caches MUST have eviction policies; unbounded caches are forbidden
File and network handles MUST be released promptly after use
Operations MUST NOT allow small inputs to trigger disproportionate resource consumption
Resource allocation MUST be throttled with configurable limits
Resource allocation inside loops MUST be minimised; pre-allocate where possible
Mutable default arguments MUST NOT be used; create fresh instances per call
Memory allocation MUST NOT use excessively large size values from untrusted input
Batch operations MUST be preferred over individual operations for bulk data
Background tasks MUST be offloaded from request-handling threads
Memory-intensive operations MUST use streaming instead of loading entire datasets
Thread/coroutine pools MUST have bounded sizes with configurable limits
Recursive algorithms MUST have depth limits or use iterative alternatives
